@@ .seajelly/migrations/v0.1.6.sql
+ALTER TABLE public.channels
+  DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS channels_platform_check;
+
+ALTER TABLE public.channels
+  ADD CONSTRAINT channels_platform_check
+  CHECK (platform IN ('telegram','wecom','feishu','slack','qqbot','whatsapp','dingtalk','discord','web','weixin'));
+
+ALTER TABLE public.events
+  DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS events_source_check;
+
+ALTER TABLE public.events
+  ADD CONSTRAINT events_source_check
+  CHECK (source IN ('telegram','wecom','feishu','slack','qqbot','whatsapp','weixin','dingtalk','discord','cron','webhook','manual'));
+

@@ release-notes/v0.1.6.md
+## Changes
+
+- Added official withdrawn-release policy support so SEAJelly can mark a bad release as removed from the upgrade path without rewriting git history
+- Added bridge-release support in the updater so future releases can reason about safe predecessor tags more explicitly
+- Published `v0.1.6` as the bridge fix for installs still on `v0.1.3`, carrying forward the `v0.1.4` database change plus the `v0.1.5` Updates-page repair
+
+## Notes
+
+- `v0.1.4` is now considered withdrawn from the official upgrade path
+- Fresh `v0.1.3` installs should upgrade directly to `v0.1.6`
+- Installs already stuck on `v0.1.4` waiting for a database update still need one manual recovery action to finish that paused run
+

@@ release-notes/v0.1.7.md
+## Changes
+
+- Published `v0.1.7` as the companion bridge release for installs that were already on `v0.1.5`
+- Carried forward the withdrawn-release policy and bridge-path updater logic introduced in `v0.1.6`, without adding a new database step
+
+## Notes
+
+- This release is intended for installs currently on `v0.1.5`
+- Installs already on `v0.1.6` stay on the same code line and should wait for the next general release
+- `v0.1.4` remains withdrawn from the official upgrade path
+

+- `previous_supported_tags` only includes source releases that still match this manifest's expected file bases

@@ pnpm tsx scripts/updater/generate-upgrade-manifest.ts \
   --db-summary "Create update_runs table"
 ```
 
+## Bridge releases and withdrawn releases
+
+If you need to skip over a bad official release:
+
+1. update `.seajelly/release-policy.json` on `main`
+2. add the bad tag to `withdrawn_releases`
+3. publish a new bridge release whose manifest points back to the last good source release
+
+Example:
+
+- `v0.1.4` is bad
+- last good public release is `v0.1.3`
+- fixed bridge release will be `v0.1.6`
+
+Generate the manifest like this:
+
+```bash
+pnpm tsx scripts/updater/generate-upgrade-manifest.ts \
+  --from v0.1.3 \
+  --release-tag v0.1.6 \
+  --db-mode manual_apply \
+  --db-sql-path .seajelly/migrations/v0.1.6.sql \
+  --db-summary "Carry forward the v0.1.4 channel constraint update"
+```
+
+That means:
+
+- older updaters that only understand `previous_supported_tag` can still move from `v0.1.3`
+- the withdrawn `v0.1.4` release can stay in git history without staying on the public upgrade path
+
+If you later want one release to support multiple source versions, make sure the expected file bases really line up for every listed source tag. `previous_supported_tags` only controls path selection; it does not magically make one patch set fit incompatible source trees.
+

notes_md:
## Changes

- Published `v0.1.7` as the companion bridge release for installs that were already on `v0.1.5`
- Carried forward the withdrawn-release policy and bridge-path updater logic introduced in `v0.1.6`, without adding a new database step

## Notes

- This release is intended for installs currently on `v0.1.5`
- Installs already on `v0.1.6` stay on the same code line and should wait for the next general release
- `v0.1.4` remains withdrawn from the official upgrade path
